column1: What is the primary purpose of the $<!DOCTYPE html>$ declaration?
column2: It instructs the browser to render the page in standards mode rather than quirks mode.

column1: Which specific issue occurs when HTML elements are closed in the wrong order (e.g., $text$)?
column2: Incorrectly nested elements, which can lead to rendering errors or broken layouts.

column1: Why is it essential to include an $alt$ attribute on every $$ tag?
column2: It provides alternative text for screen readers (accessibility) and for search engines to index image content (SEO).

column1: Which CSS-based alternative should replace deprecated tags like $<font>$ or $<center>$?
column2: CSS classes or inline styles using the $style$ attribute.

column1: What is the negative impact of using inline styles instead of an external CSS file?
column2: It makes the code difficult to maintain and prevents the reuse of styles across multiple pages.

column1: Which HTML5 attribute should be used to link a form input to its descriptive text for better accessibility?
column2: The $for$ attribute on the $<label>$ element, matching the input's $id$.

column1: Term: Divitis
column2: Definition: The practice of overusing generic $<div>$ tags for structure instead of using semantic HTML elements.

column1: Which semantic HTML5 tags should replace generic $<div>$ elements for defining the layout structure?
column2: $<header>$, $<main>$, $<article>$, $<section>$, and $<footer>$.

column1: What tool is the industry standard for identifying syntax and structural errors in HTML markup?
column2: The W3C Markup Validation Service.

column1: How do linters like HTMLHint assist developers during the coding process?
column2: They automatically flag errors like missing attributes or unclosed tags in real-time within the code editor.

column1: In what year did Tim Berners-Lee launch the first version of HTML (1.0)?
What was the primary limitation of HTML 1.0 and 2.0 regarding content types?
column2: They were focused on text-based websites and lacked support for multimedia like images and videos.

column1: Which version of HTML, released in 1997, first introduced support for tables and applets?
column2: HTML 3.2

column1: Which major enhancement was introduced in HTML 4.0 to allow dynamic modification of document structure?
column2: The Document Object Model (DOM).

column1: When was HTML 5.0 officially introduced as the latest standard?
column2: October 2014

column1: Which obsolete third-party application was rendered unnecessary for coding multimedia by the introduction of HTML5?
column2: Adobe Flash

column1: What is a key syntactic difference between HTML and XHTML regarding tag management?
column2: In XHTML, all tags must be properly closed, whereas some tags can remain open in certain HTML versions.

column1: Definition: Document Object Model (DOM)
column2: An interface that allows programming languages to manipulate the content, structure, and style of a website document as a tree of objects.

column1: Which built-in JavaScript object provides properties and methods to access and modify the website's DOM?
column2: The $document$ object.

column1: What happens to client-side JavaScript modifications to the DOM if the user refreshes the page?
column2: The changes disappear and the page reverts to its original HTML source code.

column1: Describe an instance where the browser-generated DOM differs from the original HTML source code.
column2: When the browser automatically corrects errors, such as adding a missing $<tbody>$ tag inside a $<table>$.

column1: In the DOM tree, what are the three most common types of nodes?
column2: Element nodes, Text nodes, and Comment nodes.

column1: What is the numerical $nodeType$ value for an $ELEMENT\_NODE$?
What is the numerical $nodeType$ value for a $TEXT\_NODE$?
What is the numerical $nodeType$ value for a $COMMENT\_NODE$?
Which property can be used to retrieve the tag name of an element node in the DOM?
column2: The $nodeName$ property.

column1: In browser Developer Tools, which shorthand syntax allows you to access the currently selected DOM element in the console?
Which JavaScript method is the most efficient way to access a single, unique element in the DOM?
column2: document.getElementById()

column1: What type of object is returned by the $getElementsByClassName()$ method?
column2: An array-like object called an HTMLCollection.

column1: How must you apply a style change to multiple elements returned by $getElementsByClassName()$?
column2: By using a $for$ loop to iterate through the collection and applying the style to each index.

column1: Which DOM method returns only the first element that matches a specified CSS selector?
column2: document.querySelector()

column1: What is the primary advantage of using $querySelectorAll()$ over older methods like $getElementsByTagName()$?
column2: It returns a NodeList, which supports modern iteration methods like $forEach()$.

column1: When using $querySelectorAll()$, what do comma-separated values in the selector string (e.g., $'div, p'$) act as?
column2: An "AND" operator that selects all instances of both types in the document.

column1: Which global object represents the browser tab and acts as the parent to the $document$ object?
column2: The $window$ object.

column1: Which property accesses the root $<html>$ element of the document?
column2: document.documentElement

column1: What is the difference between $parentNode$ and $parentElement$ when the parent is the document node?
column2: $parentNode$ returns the document node, while $parentElement$ returns $null$.

column1: Why does the $childNodes$ property often return more nodes than expected in an HTML-generated DOM?
column2: It counts the whitespace and indentation between elements as text nodes.

column1: Which property should be used to retrieve only the element children of a node, excluding text and comments?
column2: The $children$ property.

column1: How do $firstElementChild$ and $lastElementChild$ differ from $firstChild$ and $lastChild$?
column2: They specifically target the first or last element nodes, ignoring text nodes like whitespace.

column1: Which property is used to access the node that immediately follows a specified node on the same tree level?
column2: The $nextSibling$ property.

column1: Which DOM method creates a new element node but does not yet insert it into the document?
column2: document.createElement()

column1: What is the risk associated with using $innerHTML$ to add content to an element?
column2: It presents a cross-site scripting (XSS) risk because it can execute inline JavaScript.

column1: Which property is the safer alternative to $innerHTML$ for setting the text content of a node?
column2: The $textContent$ property.

column1: Which method is used to add a new node as the last child of a specified parent?
column2: node.appendChild()

column1: What are the two arguments required by the $insertBefore()$ method?
column2: The new node to be added and the sibling node that will follow the new node.

column1: Which method replaces an existing child node with a new node?
column2: node.replaceChild(newNode, oldNode)

column1: How does $node.remove()$ differ from $node.removeChild()$?
column2: $remove()$ is called directly on the node to be deleted, whereas $removeChild()$ is called on the parent to delete a child.

column1: Which method returns a boolean indicating whether an element possesses a specific attribute?
column2: hasAttribute()

column1: Which method is used to retrieve the value of a specific attribute, such as an image's source?
column2: getAttribute()

column1: How do you remove an attribute, such as $href$, from an anchor tag using JavaScript?
column2: element.removeAttribute('href')

column1: Which property allows you to overwrite the entire class attribute of an element as a single string?
column2: The $className$ property.

column1: Which $classList$ method adds a class to an element without overwriting existing classes?
column2: classList.add()

column1: What is the function of the $classList.toggle()$ method?
column2: It adds a class if it is not present and removes it if it is already present.

column1: Which method checks if a specific class value exists on an element and returns true or false?
column2: classList.contains()

column1: Why must kebab-case CSS properties (like $background-color$) be changed when used in the JavaScript $style$ property?
column2: JavaScript uses camelCase notation (e.g., $backgroundColor$) for style properties.

column1: What is a significant drawback of using $element.setAttribute('style', ...)$ to modify styles?
column2: It removes all existing inline styles on the element and replaces them with the new string.

column1: Definition: Event Listener
column2: A responsive interface attached to an element that waits for a specific action (like a click) to trigger a callback function.

column1: Why are inline event handlers (e.g., $onclick="..."$) generally discouraged in modern development?
column2: They mix logic with markup, making the code harder to maintain and scale.

column1: What happens if you assign two separate functions to the $onclick$ property of the same element?
column2: The second function will overwrite the first one, and only the second will fire.

column1: Which method allows multiple functions to be triggered by the same event on a single element?
column2: addEventListener()

column1: What are the two mandatory parameters for $addEventListener()$?
column2: The event type (as a string) and the listener callback function.

column1: When passing a named function to $addEventListener()$, why are parentheses omitted after the function name?
column2: Because you are passing a reference to the function rather than invoking it immediately.

column1: Which method is used to stop an element from responding to a specific event that was previously added?
column2: removeEventListener()

column1: Which mouse event fires specifically when an element is clicked twice in rapid succession?
column2: dblclick

column1: How do $mouseenter$ and $mouseleave$ events differ from $click$ events?
column2: They are triggered by the position of the mouse pointer relative to the element's boundaries rather than a button press.

column1: Which browser tool provides an accessibility score and a report on whether a site meets WCAG standards?
column2: Lighthouse (within Chrome DevTools).

column1: What is the primary benefit of using semantic HTML5 tags for SEO?
column2: They help search engine crawlers understand the structure and priority of content on a webpage.

column1: In the context of the DOM tree, what is a 'descendant'?
column2: Any node nested more than one level below a specified parent node.

column1: Which tool can be integrated into a CI/CD pipeline to ensure invalid HTML does not reach production?
column2: CLI tools like $html-validate$ or $htmlhint-cli$.

column1: What is the recommended indentation for readable and maintainable HTML code?
column2: 2 or 4 spaces.

column1: How does the browser handle a missing required $<tbody>$ tag in a $<table>$ source?
column2: It automatically inserts the $<tbody>$ tag into the DOM during parsing.

column1: Which JavaScript property can be used to check if a node has any children at all?
column2: The $hasChildNodes()$ method or checking the $length$ of the $childNodes$ property.

column1: Which DOM property allows you to see the exact text content inside a text node or a comment node?
column2: The $nodeValue$ property.

column1: What logic does $querySelector('div, article')$ follow if both elements exist on the page?
column2: It returns the one that appears first in the document.

column1: Why is it important to test websites using keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter)?
column2: To ensure the site is usable for individuals with motor impairments who cannot use a mouse.

column1: Which HTML version first introduced the support for scripting languages like JavaScript?
column2: HTML 4.0

column1: In modern development, why is it preferred to work with the DOM directly rather than using libraries like jQuery?
column2: Modern browsers have standardized DOM APIs that are fast and efficient, reducing the need for external library overhead.

column1: What specific character is used as a selector for an ID attribute in $querySelector()$?
column2: The hash symbol ($#$).

column1: What specific character is used as a selector for a class attribute in $querySelector()$?
column2: The period or full stop ($.$).

column1: Which method is used to swap an existing CSS class for a different one in a single step?
column2: classList.replace('oldClass', 'newClass')
